home.name: Catharina van der Boor
home.lede: Assistant Professor in Mental Health of Forcibly Displaced Populations at the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine and Co-Director of the Centre for Health in Humanitarian Crises. I work on designing and evaluating mental health interventions for populations affected by conflict and forced displacement. I also work with UN agencies and NGOs as an independent consultant.
anchors: Fieldwork in Uganda, Ukraine, Colombia, Sweden
Consultancy work for WHO, International Medical Corps, Mid Sweden University, University of Cambridge

work.title: MHPSS trial — Uganda and Ukraine
work.body: An effectiveness and implementation trial of a mental health and psychosocial intervention for populations affected by conflict and forced displacement.

work.title: Indigenous Mental Health and Wellbeing — Colombia
work.body: Co-developing and validating the first mental health and wellbeing scale with the conflict-affected Kankuamo Indigenous community in Colombia.

work.title: MHPSS Needs of Children and Adolescents in Ukraine
work.body: Assessing the mental health and psychosocial needs and available support services for children and adolescents living on the frontline of Ukraine.

home.closing: Available for consultancy on MHPSS programme design, cultural adaptation of instruments, needs and service availability assessments, and implementation evaluation.

about.title: About
about.headshotAlt: Headshot of Catharina van der Boor
bio: Hi there, my name is Carine (short for Catharina). For the last eight years I've worked in global mental health, focusing on people who have been forcibly displaced by conflict or disaster. I care deeply about social justice and about decolonising research: working with affected communities rather than studying them, and designing projects around the needs they identify themselves. That commitment shapes both how I work and what I work on. When I take on a project, I bring the same motivation to it: careful methods, honest answers, and a real dedication to the people the work is meant to serve.

timeline.role: Director of the Psychology Programme, Universitat Carlemany
Co-Director, Centre for Health in Humanitarian Crises, LSHTM
Senior Lecturer in Global Mental Health, LSHTM
Research Fellow in Global Mental Health, LSHTM
Postdoctoral Researcher in Global Mental Health, University of Liverpool
Research Data Coordinator, Médecins du Monde (United Kingdom)
MHPSS Programme Developer, Fundación Vicente Ferrer (India)

education.degree: PhD in Global Mental Health, University of Liverpool
education.detail: Thesis: "The Mental Health and Wellbeing of Asylum Seeking and Refugee Populations in High-Income Countries: A Capabilities Approach."

education.degree: MSc in Global Mental Health, University of Glasgow
BSc in Psychology and Neuroscience, Utrecht University

leadership: Associate Editor of Conflict and Health (2025–present); expert reviewer for The Lancet Psychiatry, Global Mental Health, Trials, and Social Psychiatry and Psychiatric Epidemiology.
Co-founder and board member of the Mental Health and Psychosocial Research Network.

research.title: Research
research.metaDescription: Research on forced displacement, MHPSS, and implementation science. Current projects in Uganda, Ukraine, and Colombia.
research.intro: My research sits at the intersection of forced displacement, mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS), and implementation science. I develop and evaluate culturally adapted diagnostic instruments and scalable psychological interventions for populations affected by conflict, displacement, and structural adversity, with a particular interest in how interventions perform when delivered at scale in low-resource and humanitarian settings.

projects.title: MHPSS trial — Uganda and Ukraine
projects.body: A multi-country trial evaluating a mental health and psychosocial support intervention for populations affected by conflict and displacement, with sites in Uganda and Ukraine.

projects.title: Kankuamo Wellbeing Scale — Colombia (£300k, UKRI / Minciencias)
projects.body: Cultural adaptation and psychometric validation of a wellbeing instrument co-developed with the Kankuamo Indigenous community, funded by UKRI in partnership with Minciencias.

projects.title: SPIDR consortium — Horizon Europe (under evaluation)
projects.body: Multi-partner European research consortium on mental health and psychosocial support for displaced populations. Proposal currently under evaluation by Horizon Europe.

research.methodsBody: My work combines mixed methods, psychometric validation, systematic reviews, participatory action research, multivariable regression, and exploratory and principal component analysis (EFA / PCA).

consultancy.title: Consultancy
consultancy.metaDescription: Consultancy on MHPSS programme design, evaluation, cultural adaptation of instruments, and capacity building for UN agencies, INGOs, and ministries of health.
consultancy.intro: I advise UN agencies, international NGOs, ministries of health, and academic partners on the design and evaluation of mental health and psychosocial support programmes. I support the cultural adaptation and psychometric validation of measurement instruments, conduct needs assessments and implementation evaluations in conflict- and displacement-affected settings, and deliver training and capacity-building for research and programme teams. My engagements draw on the same methodological standards as my academic work — mixed methods, psychometric rigour, and an implementation science lens.

engagements.title: Short-term advisory
engagements.body: Targeted technical input on MHPSS programme design, study design, and methodological choices for time-bounded questions.

engagements.title: Evaluation and needs assessment
engagements.body: Mixed-methods evaluations and needs assessments in humanitarian and displacement-affected settings, from protocol to final report.

engagements.title: Training and capacity building
engagements.body: Training of research and programme teams in psychometric validation, qualitative methods, and implementation evaluation.

publications.title: Publications
publications.lead: Peer-reviewed publications across Trials, Transcultural Psychiatry, Conflict and Health, Global Mental Health, BMC Public Health, and others, plus a technical review for Cambridge University Press.
